summary.quantification_method: relative quantitation calculations using the recorded ion intensities (area under the curve (AUC))

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: The present study aims at investigating the effects that two commonly used types of burial practices (entombment and inhumation) have on bone protein survival.

The samples analyzed are divided into two cohorts, one being corpses buried in zinc-lined coffins and placed in mausoleums in cemeteries (entombed) and the other being bodies placed in wooden coffins and buried in the ground (inhumed).

attributes.route_or_context: zinc-lined coffins in mausoleums vs wooden coffins in ground
evidence: The samples analyzed are divided into two cohorts, one being corpses buried in zinc-lined coffins and placed in mausoleums in cemeteries (entombed) and the other being bodies placed in wooden coffins and buried in the ground (inhumed).
The sample consists of 14 exhumed individuals from cemeteries in Southern Italy with different AADs (29–85 years) and PMIs (1–37 years).

replicate_hints: processed in duplicate (“A” and “B” samples) for protein extraction
robust empirical Bayes regression (ComBat) was applied to normalize the batch effects between A and B biological replicates

evidence: t-test using the burial condition as the grouping variable allowed for the identification of 20 NCPs whose relative abundances are significantly different between the two groups

quantification_strategy: relative quantitation calculations using the recorded ion intensities (area under the curve (AUC))

base_candidates_ranked.reason: Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) is explicitly described ('top 20 most abundant ions were selected for data-dependent MS/MS analysis') and relative quantification was performed using ion intensities (AUC) without labeling.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The study specifically focuses on peptide deamidation as a marker for age and PMI, calculating modification percentages for specific peptides.
